      "title": "Incorrect subdomain comparison logic",
      "description": "Line 143 compares split domain parts using `==` but the logic is flawed. When both `website_host` and `discourse_host` have the same number of parts AND length > 2, the code compares `website_host.split('.')[1..-1]` with `discourse_host.split('.')[1..-1]`. However, this assumes both have the same structure. For example, if website_host='sub.example.com' (3 parts) and discourse_host='forums.example.com' (3 parts), both pass the length check, but the comparison should work. The real issue: if website_host has MORE parts than discourse_host (e.g., 'deep.sub.example.com' vs 'forums.example.com'), the length check fails and falls through to the final else clause, which then uses `ends_with?` - this can produce false positives (e.g., 'xample.com' would match '.example.com').",
      "suggestion": "Rewrite the subdomain logic to properly extract the apex domain from both hostnames and compare, or use a dedicated library for domain comparison. The `ends_with?` check on line 147 is particularly dangerous as it can match partial domain names."
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "category": "URI Handling",
      "file": "app/serializers/user_serializer.rb",
      "line": 141,
      "title": "Potential nil dereference in path extraction",
      "description": "The code calls `URI(website.to_s).path` multiple times (lines 141, 143, 149) without explicit nil checking after the rescue on line 139. While the rescue handles parse failures, `URI.path` could theoretically return nil or an empty string. If `path` is nil, concatenating it with `website_host +` will produce unexpected results.",
      "suggestion": "Explicitly handle the path result: `(URI(website.to_s).path || '').empty? ? website_host : website_host + URI(website.to_s).path` or extract path to a variable once."
